input.background: Each year, cardiologists surgically repair thousands of clogged coronary arteries only to have many of these arteries narrow again (restenose) soon after surgery. A study sponsored by the NIH was conducted to help determine whether infection with a common type of virus, cytomegalovirus (CMY), contributed to coronary restenosis. Forty-nine of the subjects showed serological evidence of CMV infection, while 26 showed no such evidence. In the CMV+ group, 21 individuals restenosed within 6 months of atherectomy. In the CMV — group, 2 individuals restenosed.
input.question: What are the results calculated by WinPepi Compare2.exe for the confidence intervals using different methods?

output.answer:
DIFFERENCE $[\mathrm{A}$ minus $B]=0.352 \mathrm{~S} . \mathrm{E} .=0.088$

Large-sample method (Fleiss), continuity-corrected:

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 90 \% \text { C.I. }=0.178 \text { to } 0.526 \\
& 95 \% \text { C.I. }=0.150 \text { to } 0.553 \\
& 99 \% \text { C.I. }=0.096 \text { to } 0.608
\end{aligned}
$$

Wilson's score method:
Not continuity-corrected (Newcombe's method 10):

$$
90 \% \text { C.I. }=0.181 \text { to } 0.479
$$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 95 \% \text { C.I. }=\mathbf{0 . 1 4 3} \text { to } \mathbf{0 . 5 0 1} \\
& 99 \% \text { C.I. }=0.067 \text { to } 0.542
\end{aligned}
$$

Continuity-corrected (Newcombe's method 11):

$$
\begin{aligned}
& 90 \% \text { C.I. }=0.156 \text { to } 0.492 \\
& 95 \% \text { C.I. }=0.118 \text { to } 0.513 \\
& 99 \% \text { C.I. }=0.042 \text { to } 0.552
\end{aligned}
$$

The $95 \%$ confidence interval by the Wilson score method, not continuity-corrected (bold face) most closely corresponds with our plus-four method.
